sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented in available sources

special_mechanics.name: Double Jump
special_mechanics.description: Epsilon-Eagle can perform a double jump to gain additional air height

special_mechanics.name: Hover
special_mechanics.description: Epsilon-Eagle can hover in the air; limited to one hover per jump sequence

special_mechanics.name: Dash
special_mechanics.description: Epsilon-Eagle can dash across the screen

special_mechanics.name: Phoenix Force
special_mechanics.description: At full health, dash becomes Phoenix Force - a deadly move that damages enemies in its path. Consumes 50 HP. Causes slowmotion when hitting targets. Phoenix persists when directed at screen edge and continues to burn in place, dealing cumulative damage to all parts. Terminated immediately when hitting obstacles other than screen edge.

special_mechanics.name: Counter Force
special_mechanics.description: Timed counter move that converts enemy bullets into health. Can be used to remain airborne, gain elevation, and move around in air. Capable of double counter force in quick succession by performing in air and landing before animation completion.

special_mechanics.name: Zero Teleport
special_mechanics.description: Zero force fired when out of ammo. Very weak, very short range. When performed at full health, triggers Phoenix transformation.

tier_notes: Tier 2 empirical/qualitative data. Sources document gameplay mechanics and special moves but contain no precise physics values like acceleration, gravity, or sub-pixel measurements. Movement parameters are mechanically described (can run, double jump, hover, dash) but lack quantitative data. Game designed for hardcore players with emphasis on boss fights and combat rather than precise platforming physics.
